message:
test(fuzz): add CanonicalSerializer schema-binding rejection-gate fuzz target (resolves cchj5)

CanonicalSerializer (lib.rs:263-376) envelopes canonical CBOR with a
32-byte schema hash prefix and runs five distinct rejection gates on
deserialize. The deserialize_unchecked path skips NonCanonicalEncoding —
the asymmetry IS the security boundary.

Existing canonical_cbor target covers panic-freedom and round-trip
under matching schema, but NOT the rejection-gate metamorphic
relations.

Six properties:
- truncated-prefix rejection: input shorter than SCHEMA_HASH_LEN MUST
  fail with MissingSchemaHashPrefix
- round-trip byte stability: serialize→deserialize→serialize identity
- deserialize ⇒ deserialize_unchecked refinement: stricter gate accepts
  imply weaker gate accepts and same decoded value
- schema-prefix bit-flip rejection (any byte) → SchemaMismatch
- cross-schema rejection: bytes under SchemaA MUST NOT verify under
  SchemaB (load-bearing for SchemaId::hash injectivity, mzi9x)
- trailing-bytes rejection (NonCanonical or TrailingBytes)

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) byte-0 bit-flip on a known prefix MUST trip SchemaMismatch
- (b) SchemaA↔SchemaB cross-verify MUST trip SchemaMismatch (mzi9x
  regression anchor — SchemaId::hash length-prefixing fix)
- acceptance anchor: clean round-trip under matching schema MUST
  succeed (otherwise rejection anchors are vacuous)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -511,5 +511,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_canonical_serializer_schema_binding"
+path = "fuzz_targets/canonical_serializer_schema_binding.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
